page: Microsoft Azure E2pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Epdsv6 server family. It is powered by a dedicated Microsoft Neoverse-N2 CPU operating at 3.4 GHz, featuring 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and a large 128 MB L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It also bundles 118 GB of local SSD storage and supports Premium Storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the average range. Single-threaded gzip compression is a notable strength, ranking in the top 25% of tested systems. However, multi-core and multi-threaded operations, such as Geekbench multi-core, bzip3 decompression, and stress-ng multi-core, are weak. Memory performance is mixed, with large-block read bandwidth achieving average speeds of 45.87 GB/sec, while write bandwidth and small-block read bandwidth remain weak.

The E2pds_v6 exhibits poor performance in cryptographic operations, Passmark CPU tests, and LLM inference tasks. These results highlight a clear tradeoff: while the instance offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated physical cores, it is not suited for compute-intensive, parallelized, or cryptographic workloads. Qualitatively, the server provides a cost-efficient entry point for memory-sensitive workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. It is best suited for lightweight database operations, caching layers, text processing, and basic web serving where a larger memory footprint is required relative to compute power.
description: Microsoft Azure E2pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Epdsv6 family, featuring a dedicated 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor with 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and includes 118 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage support. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded gzip compression, though multi-core execution, memory write bandwidth, and cryptographic operations are weak. The instance presents a clear tradeoff, offering a generous memory allocation relative to its compute capacity, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound but compute-light tasks. It is best suited for lightweight databases, caching layers, text processing, and basic web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Neoverse-N2 vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and 118 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-sensitive, low-compute workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E2pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 118 GB local storage, designed for memory-optimized workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine offering a high memory-to-core ratio and local SSD storage for memory-sensitive, lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: ARM64 architecture with 2 dedicated Microsoft Neoverse-N2 vCPUs at 3.4 GHz
16.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU resource density
118 GB of bundled local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability
Weak multi-core, cryptographic, and LLM inference capabilities
Best suited for lightweight databases, caching, and text processing
